def: s.m. The circumference or environs of the mouth; — (Gr. νοῦς), intellect, judgment, understanding.
more: s. m. 1. The environs of the mouth. 2. Understanding. (Gr. νοῦς ).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀡𑀲𑀸 (ṇasā), from Sanskrit स्नसा (snasā).

ety: rel. n. fr. Nuṣair, a proper name, and dim. of naṣr, q.v.
def: s.m. Name of a sect (founded by Nusair) who believe in the divinity of the Khalīfa ʻAlī; — an individual of that sect; — name of a tribe inhabiting various parts of Syria and Asia Minor.
more: s. m. A mahometan sectary who believes in the divinity of Aﻌlī.

ety: inf. n. of نشر; see nashr
def: s.m. Rising from the dead, reviving; — recalling to life, raising from the dead; resurrection
more: (from نشر) s. m. Raising of the dead, the resurrection.
subs: 'The rising and the resurrection,' the day of resurrection' (i.q. yaumuʼn-nushūr).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نُشُور (nušūr), from Arabic نُشُور (nušūr).

ety: for A. نسخة nusḵẖat, fr. نسخ 'to copy.' &c.
def: s.m. A copy or model (whence anything is taken), an exemplar, a prototype; a manuscript-copy, a copy, an edition; a copy-book, writing-book; — a recipe, prescription (of medicine, or of ingredients for any composition)
more: n. m. نسخ transcribing. 1. A copy; an edition. Double entendre. Aë sīm-badan! din hai, is vaqt bhalā kyā dūṅ? Shab hone de, nuskhā tujhe sone kā batā dūṅ! It is broad day, what can I give you now, fair belle? At night a sleeping draught I'll give, you shall sleep well! 2. A recipe; prescription. nuskhā bāṅdhnā . To dispense a prescription. nuskhah likhnā , v. a. To write a prescription.

(from نسخ) s. m. 1. A recipe, prescription of ingredients for any composition. 2. An exemplar, prototype, a copy or model. 3. A writing book. Plur. نسخجات nusḵẖajāt, Books. نسخه نويسي nusḵẖa-navīs-ī, Recipe-writing.
subs: (-kā), To write a recipe or prescription
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نُسْخَه (nusxa), borrowed from Arabic نُسْخَة (nusḵa).

def: s.m. pl. (of nusḵẖa, after the model of the reg. fem. pl. of the Ar., final silent ه being euphonically changed to j), Copies, editions; books, volumes; — recipes, prescriptions.

ety: for A. نصرة, v.n. fr. نصر; see naṣr
def: s.f. Assistance, efficient aid; defence; — victory
more: s. f. Victory. See نصر naṣr. نصرت آيات naṣrat-āyāt, adj. Victorious (standards).
subs: adj. In whose standards is victory; victorious.
ex: گندے لوگوں کو خدا کی نصرت کبھی نہیں ملتی۔
gande logõ ko xudā kī nusarat kabhi nahĩ milti.
The evil ones never obtain help from God.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نصرت (nusrat), from Arabic نُصْرَة (nuṣra).

def: s.m. pl. (of naṣṣ, q.v.), Manifestations. exhibitions; demonstrations, settings forth.
part. (f. -ā), Praised, commended, applauded, eulogized, lauded hymned.
adj. (f. -ā), New, fresh; recent, modern; young; novel, strange
subs: v.t. To render new; to renew.

def: s.f. = S نوتنتو नूतनत्व nūtanatva, s.m. Newness; freshness; novelty.
s.m. = S نوتنتا नूतनता nūtanatā, s.f. Newness; freshness; novelty.

ety: for A. نطفة nuṭfat, v.n. fr. نطف 'to flow,' &c.
def: s.m. Clear water, lymph; — seed, sperma hominis; — a son; — the sea
more: n. m. 1. Semen (بيج 4). 2. Son. nutfa-i-bint-ul-inab , lit. the seed of the daughter of the grape. A bastard begotten in a drunken fit. nutfa-i-be-tahqīq, nutfa-i-harām Misbegotten; illegitimate. nutfā jamā . Conceived (in the womb).
(from نطف Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. 1. Seed, sperma hominis. 2. The sea. 3. Clear water.
subs: 'The seed of the daughter of the grape,' a bastard begotten in a drunken fit
or nut̤fa-ě-ḥarām, adj. & s.m. Misbegotten, illegitimate; — an illegitimate child, a bastard.

def: s.f. Praise, laudation, eulogium, panegyric; worship, reverence.
s.m. Speech, articulation, pronunciation; language, discourse; power of speech; reasoning faculty.
more: n. m. The power of speech.
s. m. Speech, language, discourse, articulation, reasoning faculty.

def: adj. = nūtan.
s.m. Becoming stiff and erect (the yard).
more: (from نعظ) s. m. Erectio et intensio (penis).

def: s.m. Mourning (one dead); lamenting, bewailing; complaining; — lament; complaint.
s.m. pl. (of nāʼib, q.v.), Vicegerents, deputies, lieutenants, governors.
more: s. plur. (of نايب from نوب Heb. and Aram. ) Vicegerents, lieutenants, deputies, governours. ¶ nawwāb (adj. superl.) A nabob, a governour of a town or district.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نَوَّاب (nawwāb), from Arabic نُوَّاب (nuwwāb), plural of نَائِب (nāʔib).

ety: for A. نزهة, v.n. fr. نزه; see nazāhat
def: s.f. Distance; — spotless purity; integrity; — pleasantness (of a place); verdure, freshness; — pleasure, delight, joy, cheerfulness; ornament
more: (from نزه) s. f. 1. Verdure, freshness, pleasure, delight, cheerfulness. 2. Distance. 3. Integrity. 4. Ornament.
subs: s.f. A pleasant place, a place of delight, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian نُزْهَت (nuzhat), from Arabic نُزْهَة (nuzha).

def: s.m. Descending; alighting, sojourning (see nāzil)
more: n. m. 1. Crown or government lands; escheated land. Nuzūl-i-shāhī . 2. ( Arith .) (H. utār ; S. मूल ) Evolution. āb-i-nuzūl . A swelling of the testicle. Except in the case of a blow or of hernia it is universally ascribed to the descent of water. The term therefore comprehends hydroccle hernia humoralis , and scirrhus .

(inf. from نزل) s. m. 1. Descent, alighting, sojourning. 2. A cataract (in the eye). نزول آب nuzūl-i-āb, A swelling of the testicle, which (excepting the case of a blow, or of hernia) is universally ascribed to the descent of water: the term, therefore, comprehends hydrocele, hernia humoralis, and scirrhus.
subs: a descent; an alighting; — a cataract
(in the eye); — escheated property or land (that has lapsed to the state for want of legal claimants); crown or government lands

s.m. A swelling of the testicle, which (excepting in the case of a blow or of hernia) is universally ascribed to the descent of water (it therefore comprehends hydrocele, hernia humoralis, and schirrus).

def: s.f. Alighting in a house without the permission of the owner; — a house so entered (i.q. ḵẖāna-ě-nuzūl); — escheated property or land (i.q. nuzūl, q.v.).
s.f. Vows, promises (made to God); offerings (to God).
s.f. Vows, promises (made to God); offerings (to God).
n. m. Logic.
s.m. The Indian fig-tree, Ficus indica (syn. baṛ).
s.f. The plant Salvinia cucculata.
s. m. 1. A distributor of justice. 2. A logician.
s.m. corr. of nyāy, q.v.
s.m. 'Making low,' humiliation, humbling; — contempt, disregard, disrespect.

ety: prob. S. नियाम ( = नियम)+रः; Prk. डो
def: s.m. A tree which has sprung up of itself in a cultivated field (and which the cultivator may legally cut down).
s.m. One hundred millions.
s.m. Putting down, placing; depositing; — a deposit; a pledge; — delivering, entrusting, committing (to); delivery, consignment; — keeping (a thing) in mind.
part. Put or laid down, deposited; delivered, consigned; pledged; — accumulated, hoarded.
s.f. (dialec.) Caste; species, kind, sort.
s.m. Justice, equity, &c. (see nyāy)
more: s. m. Justice, equity, right, decision. نياوکرنا nyāw karnā, To judge, to administer justice, to decide.
subs: (-kā), To administer justice; to hear complaints; to settle a dispute
adv. For the purpose of justice

def: s.f. contrac. of next.

r: nyāya, nyāy, nyāʼe, niyāy, niyāʼe, nī, ni, i
def: s.m. Method, way, rule, model, manner, system, plan; right manner, fit or suitable method; fitness, propriety; justice, equity; virtue; truth; law, a lawful act; — policy, good government; — a law-suit; — decision (in a law-suit), adjudication, judicial sentence, judgment; arbitrament; — reason, argument, disputation, logic; proof; a complete syllogism; — the Nyāya doctrine (which consists principally of Logic); name of a Hindū school of philosophy
more: s. m. 1. Justice, equity. 2. Reason, argument, disputation, sophistry, logick. نياي شاستر nyāʼe-shāstr, s. m. Logick. نياي کرنا nyāʼe karnā, To judge, administer justice, decide.
subs: (˚ya+āć˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Acting justly, just; virtuous; — a just or virtuous man
(˚ya+ādh˚), s.m. 'Receptacle of justice,' &c.; a term applied to anyone who is a model of virtue or propriety
s.m. Decree (of a judge), decision; arbitrament
(-kā), To desire or demand justice (for); to seek redress
s.f. A court of justice, or of arbitration
s.m. The aphorisms of the Nyāya philosophy (by Gautam)
s.m. The philosophical system of the Nyāya school; a treatise on the Nyāya system; — the science of Logic (as connected with the Nyāya school)
s.m. One versed in the Nyāya system of philosophy, or in logic
s.f. A court of justice or law
s.m. (f. -trī), One who acts with justice or equity
(-kā), To administer justice; to judge, to decide; to arbitrate (for)
adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Acting rightly, behaving properly; — one who acts rightly, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā) = nyāyī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Fit, proper; right, just, equitable; rational; logical
more: s. m. A judge. नैयायिक naiyāyik, A reasoner, arguer, logician, sophist.
subs: a just or an equitable person; — an administrator of justice
a judge; an arbitrator, umpire, a referee; — a rational person.

def: s.f. = S نيايتو न्यायत्व nyāyatva, s.m. Fitness, propriety.
s.m. = S نيايتا न्यायता nyāyatā, s.f. Fitness, propriety.
adj. & s.m. Fit, proper; right, just, equitable; rational; logical
subs: a just or an equitable person; — an administrator of justice
a judge; an arbitrator, umpire, a referee; — a rational person.

subs: a just or an equitable person; — an administrator of justice
a judge; an arbitrator, umpire, a referee; — a rational person.

def: adj. Regular; fit, proper, suitable; right, just, equitable; — lawfully claimable, litigable.
s.m. (rustic or dialec.) = nihorā, q v.
s.f. (rustic or dialec.) = nihorā, q.v. s.m. Solicitation, begging (of), humble entreaty, coaxing; — favour, obligation, kindness; indebtedness; — connexion; necessity
subs: (-kā), To entreat, beseech, beg (of); — to place under an obligation; — to upbraid with favours conferred
(-kā), To consider as a favour or kindness (on the part of); to acknowledge (one's) indebtedness (to); to be thankful or grateful (for).

def: s.m. = newtā = nautā, q.v. s.m. An invitation, &c. (i.q. nautā or notā, q.v.)
subs: s.m. One who receives an invitation, a guest.

def: v.t. (dialec.) = nauṛhānā, or nihuṛānā, qq.v. v.t. To bow; to bend; — to cause to stoop; — to render submissive or obedient; to subdue.
more: v. a. (dakh.) To bow, to bend: see خمانا ḵẖamānā.

def: v.n. (dialec.) = nauṛhnā, or nihuṛnā, qq.v. v.n. To incline downwards; to bend; to stoop; — to condescend; — to be subdued; to submit, &c.
more: v. a. To bow, to bend. See نہوڙنا nihūṛnā.

def: v.n. (dialec.) = nauṛhnā, or nihuṛnā, qq.v. v.n. To incline downwards; to bend; to stoop; — to condescend; — to be subdued; to submit, &c.
more: v. n. (dakh.) To bow, bend, decline ( Harris ).

def: adj. (f. -ā), Lessened, diminished; less; lower; inferior; deficient, defective, wanting, lacking, destitute (of); deprived (of); — low, vile; wicked; despicable; blamable
subs: s.m. The state of being more or less; deficiency or excess; inequality
(˚na+an˚), adj. (f. -ī), Defective in a limb or organ; maimed, mutilated; imperfect
(˚na+in˚), adj. Deficient in some organ or sense, imperfect (as blind, deaf, &c.).

def: = S نيونانگ न्यूनांग nyūnāṅg See s.v. nyūn.
= S نيونادهك न्यूनाधिक nyūnādhik, See s.v. nyūn.
s.f. = S نيونتو न्यूनत्व nyūnatva, s.m. Deficiency; paucity; inferiority, incompleteness.
s.m. = S نيونتا न्यूनता nyūnatā, s.f. Deficiency; paucity; inferiority, incompleteness.
pron. He, she, it; that.
intj. (generally applied to inferiors), Ho! ho there! holla;
more: (To inferiors). intj. Ho! ho there! hollo!; The ninth vowel of the Hindi alphabet, and the common termination of the imperative mood, plural nouns in the vocative case, and, with ī , of nouns and adjectives in the feminine gender. It is often interchanged with v as bilāo = bilāv a he-cat. o ho , intj. An exclamation of surprise, admiration, joy, or pain.
pron. He, she, it, that: see وه wuh.
subs: s.m. The letter or sound o.

adj. (f. -ī), Tasteless, insipid, &c. = ogrā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ī), Excessive, in excess; — adv. Excessively, in an excessive degree, too much.
more: E. Rus. n. m. An inner compartment. ( koṭhrī ). Sophe, obre bāṅṭ-le ādhe, Ādhe chhappar le, bāpū . W. Rus. Song. The large and small rooms and the thatch divide, In your own half, dear Pa, apart abide.
adj. 1. In excess; excessive; too much. E. 2. Tasteless; unseasoned; unpalatable. W. Kyā obrā dāl karī haī!

def: adj. (f. -ī), Empty, void, vain light, shallow; small, little, scanty, trifling; frivo lous, nonsensical, absurd, ridiculous, silly; low, mean, base, contemptible, insignificant, bad, poor, miserable, worthless; abandoned, disgraceful, degrading
more: adj. H. u up, thā bottom. 1. Shallow; not deep; superficial; empty-headed. 2. Small; petty; slender; inconsiderable. Ochhī pūṅjī khasmeṅ khāë . Prov. A small stock swallows up the owner. (The aggregated profits are insufficient), 3. Too small or tight (clothes); narrow; scanty; narrow-minded. 4. Trifling; trivial; frivolous; insignificant. Jahāṅ baṛī sewā, tahāṅ ochhā phal . Prov. Good service little fruit. 5. Feeble; ineffective; insufficient; abortive. Ochhā hāth paṛā . 6 Silly; ridiculous; absurd. Baṛī ochhī bāt kī . 7. Paltry; mean; base; low; unworthy; low-born. 1. Yeh baṛā ochhā hai, ehsān kar-ke ukaṭtā hai . A mean fellow who reminds you of a favor conferred. 2. Ochhī lakṛī pharrās kī, be-byāre pharrāë, Ochhe ke saṅg baiṭh-ke, sughṛoṅ kī pat jāë . Prov. The light fur-branches whiz and flap without a wind, Good men their credit lose, mixed with the baser kind. 8. Disgraceful; shame…
adj. Light, of little consequence, absurd, trifling, fickle, fruitless, silly, capricious. اوچهاهونا ochhā honā, v. n. To want.
subs: v.n. To do something degrading, to disgrace ooneself, &c.
v.n. To be shallow, light, &c.; to be wanting; to want.

def: s.f. = oćhāpan, q.v. s.m. Emptiness, lightness, shallowness; levity, frivolity, absurdity, nonsense; capriciousness; insignificance, worthlessness; lowness, baseness.
s.m. Emptiness, lightness, shallowness; levity, frivolity, absurdity, nonsense; capriciousness; insignificance, worthlessness; lowness, baseness.
adj. Wet, moist, damp.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀉𑀤𑁆𑀤𑀸𑀬𑀇 (uddāyaï, “is polished, is very clear”), from an unattested Sanskrit *उद्दात (*uddāta, “cleansed, bright, clear”), from उद्- (ud-, “over, above”) + दात (dāta, “cleansed, purified”), the latter from the root दै (dai, “to purify, cleanse”).

def: s.m. Boiled rice; grain mashed and cooked with milk; food.
s.f. The plant Sida cordifolia.
s.m. A double armful (kaulī) of corn from each heap, carried away as a perquisite by the village scribe.
n. m. A perquisite of the village scribe, consisting of as much corn from each heap as he can enfold between both arms ( kaulī ).
adj. Possessing a right or title; s.m. Proprietor; one possessing power and authority; supreme ruler.
see ادهکاري adhikār-ī.
s.m. Stream, flow, stream of perspiration.
more: n. m. S. ओघ a stream. Marks of perspiration on an unwashed body. og bah'nā , v. n. To stain with the marks of filthy perspiration. Sāre badan pe og bah rahe haiṅ His whole body reeks with filthy perspiration.

def: s.m. Moisture near the surface of soil, ooze, subsoil moisture (syn. āl).
more: n. f. from gīl moisture. Subsoil moisture.

def: n. f. A Hindu festival performed on the twelfth day of Bhādoṅ badī , or the dark fortnight in August.
s.f. A Hindū festival performed (among Khattrīs) on the twelfth day of Bhādoṅ badī (syn. bhāʼī-bhinnā).
s.m. Stream, torrent; current, flow; multitude, aggregate, collection, heap.
s. m. Multitude, aggregate (in general), a collection.
s.m. A kind of gruel, &c. = ogrā, q.v.
adj. Wanting ghī or clarified butter, insipid, tasteless, unpalatable.
s.m. = ogrā, q.v.
more: s. m. A dish of food so called.
s. m. Rice gruel, commonly called by Europeans conjī ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: adj. Insipid, tasteless, &c. = obrā and oghrā, q.q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Excessive, in excess; — adv. Excessively, in an excessive degree, too much.
more: adj. H. ū out, ghī clarified butter. Without ghī or butter; unseasoned; unpalatable. Ghī wī to dete nahīṅ, ogrā khānā sāmne dhar dete haiṅ .

def: s.m. A mixed mess, a dish like khićṛī (q.v.): gruel, pottage.
intj. Oh! ha! (an exclamation of pain, distress, &c.); pshaw! &c. = ūṅh, q.v.
more: intj. 1. Never mind. See اونه 2. Oh! an exclamation of pain.

def: s.m. Covering, &c. = uhār, q.v. s.m. Covering, wrapper; cover or curtain of a cart, litter, pālkī, &c. (as a protection from sun or rain, or as a means of concealment for women: — see ohār and uhāṛ).

ety: for A. عہدة ʻǒhdat, v.n. fr. عہد; see ʻahd
def: s.m. An engagement, obligation, agreement, a contract, bargain; responsibility, suretiship, becoming security (for); a commission; an office, business, employment, duty, occupation, appointment, post, trust, charge
subs: (-se), To fulfil an engagement or obligation; to be able to accomplish an undertaking; to become free or liberated from the responsibility (of); to accomplish successfully, to come well out (of)
s.f. Fulfilment of an obligation, performance of an engagement, discharge of a duty; ability to perform, means; performance; success; freedom from a responsibility
(-kā), To obtain the office or appointment (of)
or muqarrar karnā, v.t. To appoint, to instal (in an office)
vulg. ʻǒhde-dār, adj. & s.m. Intrusted with an office or a business, employed, holding a commission; — an office-holder, an official, officer, functionary, civil or military authority

s.f. = ojhal, q.v. adj. Invisible, out of sight, screened, hidden, concealed; private; — s.f. That which renders invisible (as a) screen, partition, curtain, wall (or the like); shelter, concealment; privacy, retirement; — postpn. Under the shelter (of, -kī), from behind, behind
subs: v.t. To make invisible, to put out of sight, to hide, cause to disappear, make away with
(naz̤ar-se), To become invisible, to be hidden (from view), to disappear, conceal oneself, hide; to vanish, abscond.

def: s.m. Light, splendour, lustre; gold; manifestation; strength; vitality, vital warmth (especially in the fœtus).
s.m. Entrails, guts, stomach, paunch.
n. m. 1. The stomach; paunch; belly ( peṭ ). Ojh bhare, na rog jhaṛe . Prov Nor stomach filled nor illness cured. 2. Entrails; guts ( antṛī ).
s.m. Diviner, soothsayer, sorcerer, wizard, enchanter, conjurer, exorcist, magician; — one who pretends to cast out evil spirits, to cure snake-bites, &c., by means of charms and incantations.
more: W. n. m. S. उपाध्याय teacher, Pr. uajhhā . 1. A diviner; soothsayer; wizard; magician ( jādū-gar ); conjuror; enchanter; sorcerer; exorcist. 2. One who pretends to cure diseases, the bite of snakes, and to cast out evil spirits by means of charms or incantations. Bāp ojhā, māṅ ḍāin . E Prov. The father a wizard, the mother a witch.

def: s.f. Divination, sorcery, &c.; the profession of an ojhā.
more: n. f. The profession of an ojhā .

def: s.f. The wife of an ojhā or sorcerer; sorceress, witch, &c.

ety: S. अगोचर; cf. also oṭal, oṭ
def: adj. Invisible, out of sight, screened, hidden, concealed; private; — s.f. That which renders invisible (as a) screen, partition, curtain, wall (or the like); shelter, concealment; privacy, retirement; — postpn. Under the shelter (of, -kī), from behind, behind
more: n. f. from H. u out, jhalak appearance. 1. Shadow. See oṭ (3). 2. Screen. See āṛ (1). 1. Āṅkh ojhal, pahāṛ ojhal . Prov. To be out of sight is to be over the hills and far away. 2. Ṭaṭṭī kī ojhal shikār . Prov. Behind the fence the spoiler. ojhal honā , v. n. To conceal one's self; hide; abscond; avoid or elude observation.
s. f. Privacy, retirement: see also ارٿ oṭ. اوجهل کرنا ojhal karnā, To conceal, to screen, &c. اوجهل هونا ojhal honā, To be concealed, &c.
subs: v.t. To make invisible, to put out of sight, to hide, cause to disappear, make away with
(naz̤ar-se), To become invisible, to be hidden (from view), to disappear, conceal oneself, hide; to vanish, abscond.

def: s.f. Knock, blow, push, shove, thrust; kick (of a fighting cock)
subs: To knock, strike against, &c. ( = jhok mārnā, dhakkā mārnā).

def: E. n. m. from ujhalnā To cast up. Casting up of milk after sucking.

v.t. To pour out = ujhalnā, q.v. v.t. To pour from one vessel into another, to pour out, to empty.
more: v. a. To pour out. See ujhalnā .

def: s.m. Draught of water, &c. from the hollow of the hand; the palm hollowed to drink from (syn. ćullū).
more: n. m. from S. उदक water. 1. The palm of the hand formed into a hollow for a drink of water. Pilā de ok se, sāqī jo ham se nafrat hai, Piyāla gar nahīṅ detā, na de, sharāb to de . Zauq. 2. Sickness of stomach ( ubkāī ).
s. f. 1. Sickness at the stomach, inclination to vomit. 2. A draught of water from the hollow of the hand.
s. m. 1. House, dwelling. 2. Asylum, place of refuge.

def: s.f. Sickness of the stomach, vomiting; vomit (see ubkāʼī).
s.m. House, dwelling; asylum, refuge.
adj. (f. ī.), Inferior, bad; disagreeable, nasty,
s.m. Instruments, implements, tools, apparatus ( = auzār).
s.f. A mortar to pound grain in = ukhli, q.v. s.f. = H اکهل उखल ukhal (contrac. of ūkhal),s.m. A wooden or stone mortar for pounding rice or other grain.
more: Mag. n. f. A mortar. See ukhlī. Chaltī kā nām gāṛī 1 , gaṛī 2 kā nām okhlī . Prov. The moving's called a fixture, the unfixed fixed they call! okhlī meṅ sir denā , v. n. To put one's head in a mortar; put oneself in peril; expose oneself to danger. Okhlī meṅ sir diyā, to mūsloṅ se kyā ḍar? Prov. Having put your head in the mortar, why fear the pestle?
s. f. A mortar. See اکهلي ukhlī.

def: s.m. The esculent root Arum campanulatum (syn. sūran)
more: n. m. A pungent vegetable of which the root is boiled in acid and eaten; Amorphophallus campanulatus . Wat.
n. f. olā screen. Personal bail or security; a surety; hostage (A. yargamāl ). ol meṅ denā , v. a. To surrender a debtor to his bail till payment of the money due; to give a hostage.
s. m. 1. ( ओल r. वल Destroy, i. e. complaints) Name of a vegetable the root of which is eaten. (Arum campanulatum). 2. A hostage. ¶ आवलि āvali, s. f. Row. See اولي avalī.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَوَّل (awwal), borrowed from Arabic أَوَّل (ʔawwal).

def: s.f. Personal bail or security, surety; pledge, pawn ( = rahn); hostage
subs: v.t. To surrender a debtor to his bail till payment of the money due; to give a pledge, give se urity, give a hostage.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian اَوَّل (awwal), borrowed from Arabic أَوَّل (ʔawwal).

def: n. f. Knolecole.
s.m. Hailstone, hail; refined sugar made up into balls; — adj. Cold (as hail)
more: Māṛ. n m. from S. ओल wet. 1. Hail; hailstone. Kare kyā fāëda nā-chīz ko taqlīd achchhoṅ kī, Ki jam jāne se kuchh olā to gauhar ho nahīn saktā . Dard. 2. A round lump of loaf-sugar. ole paṛ'nā , v. n. To hail; to be overtaken by misfortune. Sir muṅḍāte hī ole paṛe . Prov. His head was no sooner shaven than it hailed. (Misfortune overtook his first venture).

adj. 1. Cold; clay-cold; icy ( baraf ); frigid. Hāth pāoṅ olā ho rahe haiṅ . 2. White; snow-white; milk-white. Phr. White as driven snow; white as a sheet. 3. Very sweet; sweet as honey; honeyed. 1. Ṭhanḍā hai barf se bhī, mīṭhā hai jaise olā, Kuchh pās hai to de jā nahīṅ pī jā rāh-i-Maulā . The Bhishtī (water-bearer)'s street cry. Delhī. Colder than ice, as sugar sweet this water is, Give ye who have; if not in God's name drink gratis . 2. Kyā kahūṅ maiṅ woh miṭhāī meṅ ki kaise nikle? Koī olā, koī misrī, koī shakkar tarbūz . On the melon. Nazīr. The sweetness of the melon's such, no words for it I find, Or loaf-sugar, or candy, or like sugar unrefined. olā honā, olā ho jānā , v. n. To become cold.
n. m. 1. Curtain; screen; shadow ( oṭ ); sidewall. 2. Secrecy; secret ( bhed ). olā kar'nā , v. n. To hide; conceal; veil.

s. m. 1. Hail. 2. The name of a kind of sweetmeat. اولاهوجانا olā hojānā, To become cold. جون سرمنڐاياتون اولي پڙي joṅ sir munḍāyā toṅ ole paṛe, I had no sooner shaved my head than a shower of hail came on. (Spoken of one who has just engaged in an enterprise which turns out unfortunate).
subs: v.n. To be or become cold
v.n. To hail; to be overtaken by misfortune (joṅ sir munḍāyā toṅ ole paṛe, 'I had no sooner shaved my head than hail fell,' — i.e. Misfortune overtook my first venture).

def: s.m. Screen, &c. = oṭ, oṭal, q.q.v.; secrecy, sec. et
s.m. Pendant; plummet.
E.; v. a. To parch.
s.f. per met. for oṭal, q.v.; and cf. olā.
s.f. Security, &c. = ol, q.v. s.m. The esculent root Arum campanulatum (syn. sūran)
s. m. Territory, dominion.
s.m. An animal that has been slaughtered and scalded to remove the hair or feathers.
s.f. Eaves of a thatched house ( = orī, q.v.)
more: Māṛ; n. f. The eaves of a thatched roof. 1. Orī ke pānī baṛerī! Bhoj. Prov. The water from the eaves rise to the ridge pole! 2. Paṛausan ke meṅh barsegā, to oltī apne bhī ṭapkegī . Prov. If it rains for my neighbour it will drop from the eaves of my thatch too. 3. Khāṅche tale hāthī chhupāūṅ, Oltī kā pānī magrī chaṛhāūṅ. Aisā ṭonā kar de, rī mā . Marriage song. Ṭonā . An elephant under a hen-coop to hide, the rain from the eaves to the ridge-pole send up, with magic power endow me, mother, (that I may rule my husband). 4. Barsai maghā jhakori jhakorī; Mor dou nain chuaīṅ jas orī . Padmāwat. The pattering rain the clouds pour pelting down in showers; The tears flow from my eyes like rain from eaves of bowers. oltiyoṅ bhaṅg chūnā, kue meṅ bhaṅg paṛnā , v. n. To be highly intoxicated with bhaṅg. Oltiyon bhaṅg chuā dī . Prov. The eaves. drop intoxicating potions. (Said of a place where the p…
s. f. The eaves of a roof.
subs: To be utterly stupefied or intoxicated with bhang.

def: s.m. The mystic name for the Hindū triad (representing the union of the three gods, viz. अ, Vishṇu; उ, Śiva; म, Brahmā); — adv. Yea, verily, so be it, amen: (om is used at the beginning and end of a reading of the Vedas, previously to any prayer, and also as an auspicious salutation)
more: n. The mystic name of the triad Vishnu, Shiv, and Brahma, consisting of three letters अ, उ, म. It is used at the beginning, and, like our Amen, at the end of prayers and holy recitations, and also at the beginning of writings or respectful salutations. oṅgkār', oṅkār , The mysterious word om , q. v.
subs: s.m. The sacred and mystical syllable om; the exclamation om
(vulg. onam-āsī, or onam), s.f. 'I prostrate myself before om (the Hindū triad) and all perfect beings'; first lesson or prayer.

def: s.m. Name of the southern coast of Arabia (from the entrance into the Persian Gulf to the straits of Bab-el-mandeb); — the sea between Ethiopia and India; the Persian Gulf ( = baḥr-ě-ʻǒmān).
s.m. The name of the second ḵẖalīfa or successor of Moḥammad; a proper name.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian عمر ('umr), taken from Arabic عُمْر (ʕumr).

def: The termination of plural nouns, used in the Nom. with ne , and in the objective with ko .

n. f. Cor. of S. ओं नमः सिध्दं I prostrate myself before om (the Hindu triad) and all the perfect beings. The first lesson or prayer. Na paṛho onā-masī, na paṛho kā, khā, ghinnā, Na paṛho byākaran, jo sab bedan kī sār hai. Rām nām jāno, jin sab kuchh paichhāno, Bhale se bhalāī, na bure se bigāṛ hai . Kabit. Dont learn the pray'r nor yet the alphabet, prythee, Nor Grammar learn, though it of learning be the key. Know only Rām through whom you'll all things come to know, No care you need for good nor evil here below.
s.m. The cord with which rafters or frames for thatching on are pulled into their places and fastened till the roof is finished.
s. m. The cord with which rafters or frames for thatching on are pulled into their places and fastened till the roof is finished.

s.m. A woman's sheet = oṛhnā, q.v. v.t. To cover with, to put on, wear; — s.m. (S. आवरण+कः), Covering, sheet, coverlet, quilt; a woman's sheet or mantle (covering the head and the upper half of the body)
more: n. m. Hin. A woman's sheet. See اورَهنا.
subs: (-kā), To remove the covering or veil (of); to expose, defame
v.t. To cover (one) with a sheet or mantle; to marry (since, among villagers, in marrying a widow a sheet or mantle is thrown over her)

(-ke), To throw (her) mantle on the neck (of), to seek justice against (in doing which, an insulted woman, in former times, used to throw her mantle round the neck of the man who insulted her, and dragged him to the king for justice).

def: s.f. A woman's sheet or mantle = oṛhnī, q.v. s.f. (dim. of oṛhnā, q.v.), A woman's sheet or mantle
subs: To exchange mantles (as a token of friendship), to pledge eternal friendship
s.f. lit. 'The breeze from a woman's mantle'; petticoat influence.

s. f. Brilliancy, brightness, beauty, elegance, lustre, polish.
subs: (-ko), To give lustre (to), to polish ( = āb ćaṛhānā).

def: s.m. A man armed with weapons or clothed with mail
more: n. m. A man armed with weapons or clothed in mail.
(from اوپ) s. m. A man armed with weapons or clothed in mail. اوپچي خانه opchī-ḵẖāna, A guard-house or post of armed men.
subs: s.m. A guardhouse, or post of armed men.

def: s.m. Polishing; polish, lustre, &c. = ōp, q.v. s.f. Shine, lustre, polish, brightness; beauty, elegance ( = āb)
subs: (-ko), To give lustre (to), to polish ( = āb ćaṛhānā).

def: s.m.f. (generally f. in the sing. and masc. in the plur.), Origin; part, side, direction, quarter; end, extremity, limit, boundary (syn. t̤araf); extreme, outside, utmost, highest point or pitch (of, -ke, — in this sense the word is generally masc. plur.); drift, tendency (as of speech); — postpn. In the direction (of), towards
more: n. f. 1. Side; flank; direction; way; quarter. 2. Partiality; bias; leaning. 3. Party; side. Ham tumhārī or hain . 4. Behalf; behoof; interest ( taraf ). Bībī, merī or se bhī lālā ko puchkāryo . Hin. Wom. Sister, caress the children for me too. 5. The highest point; zenith; climax. Bādah-kashī ke or the, aish o tarab ke chhor the . Nazīr. The sparkling cup fast circled round, With song and dance and pleasure crown'd. 6. Extremity; end; termination; close. Is samandar kā or hī nahīṅ miltā . 7. Clue; trace; key; interpretation. Inkī bāt kā or hī nahīṅ miltā . or ānā , v. n. To come to one's end; complete one's ruin. Haṅste ṭhākur, khaṅste chor, in donoṅ kā āyā or . Prov. The king who laughs and the thief who coughs are near their end, the silly aufs. or pās, ore dhore , adv. Round about. See ās pās . or kar'nā, or uṭhnā, taraf-dārī karnā , v. n. 1. To be partial to, to shew partiality. 2. …

s. f. 1. Origin. 2. Boundary, limit. 3. Way, side (ward). اس اور us or, That way, on that side, thither-ward. چارون اور chāroṅ or, On the four sides, on all sides. اورنباهنا or nibāhnā, To be constant to, to protect (for ever).
subs: (-kī), To take the part or side (of); to be partial (to)
(apnī), To perform (one's) part, to do (one's) utmost; to be constant to, to carry out to the end; to protect throughout
Neither beginning nor end; something endless
On that side, that way, thitherward
From my side, from me, in my behalf ( = merī t̤araf-se).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀅𑀯𑀭 (avara), from Sanskrit अपर (ápara, “other, different”). Cognate with Punjabi ہوْر (hor).

def: s. (dakh.) See اوٿ oṭ.
s.m. Basket; pannier.
more: s. m. A basket, a pannier.

def: Gaṛh. n. m. A basket; a pannier. Ek oṛī bīgā hai, sau oṛyā bāṛhā hai . Mag. Song. Mahādeo kā biyālā . One basket thrown, a hundred more arise.
more: W. n. m. Lack; dearth; scarcity. Naukroṅ kā ūṛā to nahīṅ paṛ gayā? Wom.

def: s.m. Cavilling, &c.
more: v. a. S. ऊर्णु to cover. 1. To put on or wear (a shawl or mantle). 2. To cover one; to draw over one (a sheet).

n. m. 1. A woman's mantle covering the head and upper half of the body. 2. A bed sheet; a covering. Sās kā oṛhnā, patoh kā bichhaunā . E. Prov. The mother-in-law's shawl, the daughter-in-law's bedding. oṛhnā utār'nā , v. a. To defame; take away one's character. oṛhnā uṛhā'nā , v. a. lit . To cover with a wrapper. To marry; take one's hand in marriage. (A mode of marrying a widow among villagers). oṛhnā bichhau'nā , n. m. Bedding and covering. oṛhnā gale meṅ ḍāl'nā , v. a. To drag to the court for justice. ☞ In former times, the insulted woman would throw her wrapper round the offending man's neck and drag him to the king for justice, her uncovered head being a symbol of the disgrace she had suffered.

def: s.m. Shield, target

def: n. f. Diminutive of oṛh'nā (1). q. v. Bhātī hai jī se bandī ko āṅchal kī oṛhnī, Oṛhūṅgī sādī maiṅ to na malmal kī oṛhnī, Maṅgvā do koī mujh ko musalsal kī oṛhnī, Maiṅ woh to oṛhne kī nahīṅ kal kī oṛhnī , Rangīn. Bājī, mujhe uṛhā do jhalājhal kī oṛhnī . Wom. A mantle richly bordered I must have, I will not wear a plain white muslin grave, A rich embroidered mantle get me—nay, I will not wear what I wore yesterday, Throw on me, sister, now a mantle gay. oṛhnī badal'nā, ḍupaṭṭā —, v. n. Wom. lit . To exchange mantles. To pledge eternal friendship; to be fast friends. oṛhnī piṭṭhī , n. f. A Hindu marriage ceremony. ☞ It consists in the bridegroom's presenting paint, ornaments, jewels, and a new suit of clothes for the bride, followed by a return present from the bride of money and a sweetmeat called ṭīkā. oṛhnī kī batās , E. n. f. lit . The wind from a woman's cloak. Petticoat influence. Orhnī kī batās lagī . Prov. He has been struck by the wind from a woman's cloak. (He is a slave to his wife).
adj. Partial; — s.m. Protector; partisan, adherent; — s.f. Eaves of a house; bank of a pond or rivulet to the water's edge (also written āʼorī).
more: (from اور) 1. adj. Partial; protector, patron. 2. s. f. The eaves of a house.

def: s.f. (dim. of oṛā, q.v.), A large basket fixed on the horizontal beam of a sugar-mill, from which the mill is fed.
s.m. Felling; hemming; side-seam, seam hem, join
more: n. m. from or end. Stitching together two breadths and then hemming. ormā kar'nā , v. a. 1. To stitch; sew; hem. 2. To beat severely; make mince meat of; to beat to a mummy ( rāëtā k .).
s. m. The name of a particular kind of sewing, stitching together two breadths.
subs: v.t. To fell; to hem; to sew a seam; — to seam (the body) by beating, to beat severely or to a mummy ( = uttū kar-denā).

def: v.t. To cover (oneself) with a shield, to ward off a blow; (also, incorrectly) = oṛhnā, q.v.
more: v. n. (dakh.) See اڙنا uṛnā. ¶ oṛnā, v. a. (dakh.) See اوڙهنا oṛhnā.

def: W. n. A drill-plough.
s.f. = oṛhnī, q.v. s.f. (dim. of oṛhnā, q.v.), A woman's sheet or mantle
subs: To exchange mantles (as a token of friendship), to pledge eternal friendship

def: s.f. Dew (syn. shabnam)
more: n. f. S. अवश्याय hoarfrost. Dew; night dew; dew drop. Kahīṅ osoṅ piyās bujhī hai? Prov. Will dew ever quench one's thirst? os paṛ'nā, os paṛ jānā , v. n. lit . To fall (dew). 1. To hang down; droop; fade; languish. 2. To be damped; become cold, dull, cheerless. Sab haṅsī chohal jātī rahī, os sī paṛ gaī . 3. To be put to shame; perspire with shame. Sach kah, sabā, guloṅ par kyoṅ os paṛ gaī hai, Shāyad ki khil-khilā-kar koī haṅsā chaman meṅ . Nasīr. Why are the flowers o'erspread with dew, O breeze, now tell me true? Perhaps some fair one's ringing laughter doth the flowers with shame bedew. os ke motī , adj. Transient (as dew drops); frail; perishable. Yeh sansār os ke motī, bilak jāt ek chhin meṅ . This world is as the pearly dew which in a moment melts away.
s. f. Dew. اوس پڙجني os paṛ jānī, To fall in value, to be deprived of importance, splendour, &c., to become less in demand than formerly.
pron. demon. sing. inflect. That, &c. See اس us.
subs: (-par), lit. 'Dew to fall (upon)'; to be blighted, to lose bloom, lustre or splendour, to be dimmed, to languish, droop, fade; to be less in demand, to fall in value
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀑𑀲𑀸 (osā), from Sanskrit अवश्या (avaśyā). Cognate with Bengali ওস (ōs).

def: v.t. To knead dough, &c. ( = gūṅdhnā).

def: s.f. A young cow or buffalo, a heifer.

n. m. A tribe of Jaiṅs , chiefly merchants and bankers. Khāne ko Oswāl, pahnne ko Sirimāl, joṛne ko Aggarwāl . Prov. In eating an Oswāl, in dress a Sirīmāl, in hoarding an Aggarwāl.

ety: S. अवाप्ति, अव+आप्+ति; or उद्+वर्ता, rt. वृत्
def: s.f. Anything to be gained, advantage, gain, profit; overplus, surplus, saving; abatement, economy; recovery (from sickness), convalescence
more: n. f. H. āvat come. 1. Gain; profit ( nafā ); advantage; return. Jahāṅ ṭake kī ot hogī, wahāṅ jāeṅge . 2 Economy; spare; frugality ( kifāyat ). 3 Overplus; surplus; saving (bachat) . 4. Relief; convalescence; recovery. ot paṛnā , v. n. 1. To make a profit; to gain; buy cheap. 2. To abate or lessen (pain); get relief. Lākhan davāī rāt maiṅ ne kar ḍārí, Kāhū se nahīṅ nek to paṛī . W. Rus. Wom. Song. ot kas'ar . Profit and loss. Is meṅ kaun sī ot kasar mārī jāë hai .

n. f. S. अड्डन shield. 1. Cover. See āṛ (1, 2). 1. Tinke (yā til) kī oṭ pahāṛ . Prov. A straw before the eye a mountain hides. 2. We gaë bālam, we gaë, gaë pahāṛan oṭ, Banjāre kī āg jūṅ, gaë silagte chhoṛ . Song. Ḍholā . There goes my husband, there, a mountain twixt us risen up as 'twere, My passion's flame yet burning flung away, as is the carrier's fire. 2. Shadow; shade; shelter. (Also āṛ ). 3. Ambush; hiding place; concealment. 4. A veil; a covering for the face. 5. Opposition; preventive. 6. A scotch; wood, etc. placed under a wheel. oṭ bandī , n. f. 1. Payment of a fixed amount for the use of each plough and pair of bullocks. 2. An estimate of the probable outturn at some future period. oṭ karnā , v. a. To conceal; screen; hide.
s. f. 1. Overplus, surplus. 2. Abatement. 3. Convalescence.
subs: Profit or benefit to accrue, to be gained

To apply a scotch (to a wheel).

s.m. One who picks or separates the seed from cotton (see oṭnā).
adj. So much; so many = uttā, utnā, q.q.v. adj. (f. -ī) and adv. That much or many, &c. (see اتنا utnā).
more: n. m. 1. A partition or side wall. Līpūṅ oṭā, mare moṭā . Prov. The burning ground I plaster, may some rich man die after. ☞ The prayer of the Achāraj Brahman who gets the rich covering in which the corpse is wrapped, besides other presents. 2. A small wall against the entrance to a house; a platform of mud. 3. A heap of mud placed on the cotton machine to keep it steady. 4. One who separates the seed from the cotton.
adj. So much, so many. اوتي مين ote meṅ, In the mean-time.
adj. (dakh.) That much: see اتنا utnā.

def: s.f. Cover, screen, &c. = oṭ
subs: To be hidden; to be lost to sight, to disappear, be missing or lost.

def: n. f. Screen. See آرَ (1). oṭal ho jānā , v. n. To be lost to view; to be missing or lost.
s.m. Picking (cotton-seeds), &c. See oṭnā.
s.m. Lip = oṅṭh, q.v. s.m. The lip (see hoṅṭh)
(local) = utnā or uttā, q.q.v. adj. (f. -ī), As much as that, that much, so much, so many
subs: adv. (emphat. of utnā), Just as much as that, exactly so much or many
adv. As many as that, so many.

def: v.t. To pick or separate the seed from cotton, to thrumb.

v. a. 1. To separate the seed from the cotton. 2. To catch (as a ball); intercept; spread out any cloth for holding grain. 3. To take upon oneself; to be responsible for. 4. To talk away whether any one listens or not. Ū aisahīṅ oṭbe karelan ( yā oṭelan ). Mag.
v.t. To cover, shelter, screen, defend, &c. = oṭ karnā; to catch (a ball, &c.), to intercept; to parry, guard; to spread out a cloth to receive grain, &c.; to take upon (oneself), be responsible for.
more: (see اوٿ) v. a. 1. To defend, to screen, to shelter. 2. To thrum, to separate the seed from cotton. 3. To catch (a ball, &c.).
v. n. (dakh.) To rise: see اٿهنا uṭhnā.

def: n. f. A machine for separating the cotton from the seed.
s.f. An instrument for separating the seeds from cotton.
adj. & adv. Having cross-threads or cross-lines; crosswise and lengthwise; vertical and horizontal; vertically and horizontally,
s.m. Air, wind; Ӕolus, the god of the wind.
A masc. affix ( = pan, panā) employed in forming abstract substantives from adjectives and substantives, as buṛhāpā, 'old age,' 'senility,' from būṛhā, 'old'; muṭāpā; 'fatness,' from moṭā, 'fat.'
more: Corresponding to the English terminations hood, ness, ship, etc. A termination forming abstract nouns masculine when added to concrete nouns and adjectives, as from būṛhā, buṛhāpā; achchhā, achchhāpan .
n. m. Z. pāë , S. पाद H. pāoṅ . A foot; In Comp . it denotes duration, or continuation; as der-pā durable.

1. (Sans. पाद ) s. m. The leg, foot: see پاٴي pāʼe. پابزنجير pā-ba-zanjīr, adj. Fettered, in chains. پاشکسته pā-shikasta, Infirm, reduced. 2. In compos. part. act. of پاٴيدن To continue; as, کم پا kam-pā, Of short duration; ديرپا der-pā, Permanent.
A termination affixed to nouns to denote the abstract quality: see H. پن pan.

def: part. (and root, of pānā, 'to get, &c.'), Having got or obtained ( = pā-ke, pā-kar).

ety: Pehl. pāi; Zend pādha; S. pāda
def: s.m. Foot; leg; foot or root of a tree, &c.
subs: s.m. Carpet spread at the entrance of a room for cleaning the feet on, door-rug

adj. & s.m. Tied by the leg; clogged, fettered, bound, restrained; encumbered (with a family, &c.); — one who is restrained or bound, a servant; a rope with which the forefeet of a horse are tied; fetters
(-kā), To be clogged or fettered, &c.; to be bound (by), be ruled or guided (by), to observe, follow, conform (to); (met.) to be married
s.f. The state of being bound, &c.; restriction, restraint, check, control; observance, practice, rule
s.m. lit. 'Kissing the feet'; adoring, worshipping, revering, paying (one's) respects to ( = qadam-bos); — pā-bos honā (-kā), To adore, worship, reverence, &c.
s.f. Kissing the feet; worship, reverence, adoration; — pā-bosī karnā (-kī), To worship, adore, &c.
s.f. 'Covering the foot'; slipper; shoe; — pā-posh-par mārnā, v.t. To make light of; to care for nothing
s.m. Change of ground or place; following quick; first stage of a journey (more com. pāʼe-turāb; syn. prasthān)
v.n. To change (one's) residence; to send baggage or other articles the first stage of a journey (on the propitious day or moment on which the traveller himself cannot begin his journey)
s.m. Trousers, long drawers (more com. pāʼe-jāma, pai jāma)
adj. lit. 'Having the feet extended'; at ease, contented, tranquil
adj. lit. 'Having the foot in the stirrup'; ready, prompt, alert
adj. & s.m. Ready to start; ready, &c. ( = pā dar rikāb); — retinue, train, followers (more com. pāʼe-rikāb)
s.m.f. (?), An ornament worn on the feet or ankles (consisting of a chain with small bells attached)
adj. lit. 'Having the legs broken'; infirm, reduced, in broken circumstances, powerless, helpless
s.m. Washing of the feet (particularly with a medicinal fluid in cases of fever)
s.m. Slip, stumble; (met.) error, blunder, sin
pā-lakṛī, s.f. Blocks of wood, or wooden saucers, placed under the feet of the bed to raise it and give it a sloping position
adj. Trodden under foot, crushed, ruined, destroyed (more com. pāʼe-māl); — pā-māl karnā, v.t. To trample on; to destroy; to lay waste
(-kā), To be trampled on (by); to be ruined, &c.
s.f. Beating down by treading, destruction, ruin, devastation
s.m. A species of pigeon (having the legs covered with feathers)
adj. & s.m. Within (one's) depth, fordable; — a ford; — pā-yāb utarnā, v.n. To cross by fording, to ford (a river, &c.)

def: part. (of pāʼīdan, 'to last,' &c.; see pā above), Lasting, continuing, standing (used only as last member of comp., e.g. kam-pā, 'Of short duration'; der-pā, 'Lasting, durable, permanent').

conj. = par, q.v. s.m. & adv. (gov. gen. with -ke, if the postp. is expressed), Further bank, or opposite shore or side (of a river, lake, &c.); the other side, concluding bound; the end or limit of anything, termination, conclusion; utmost limit or extent or reach; bottom, innermost depth, inmost recess; first turning up or ploughing of land; — over, across, on or to the other or further side, through, beyond
more: conj. adv. or postpos. (contraction of پر par, q. v.) But, yet, on, at, &c. ¶ pu ( उपरि ) postpos. On, &c. see پر.
subs: v.t. To put or take across, to put or ferry over; to carry through, cause to reach the end, &c. (see next)
v.n. To go or get across (esp. the ocean of existence), to cross; to reach the end; to accomplish, get through; to succeed, gain one's end
v.n. To get to the bottom (of), to fathom, to sound
v.n. To come to an end, be over

v.t. To cross over, to ferry across or over; to pierce, go through and through, transfix; to carry through, finish, accomplish, get to the end (of, -ke); to relieve; to succeed, win, gain (in gambling)

pārag, adj. & s.m. Going to the opposite shore, crossing over, going through or over or across or to the end; intending to cross; completely mastering, thoroughly well versed in, knowing, clever, skilful, expert, able; — one who has arrived at the end, &c.

part. adj. & s.m. Passed over in safety, crossed; passed beyond the world; pure, holy; — one who has reached the opposite shore; one who has passed beyond the world; (with Jains) an Arhat or deified saint or teacher

pār honā or ho-jānā, v.n. To be carried across, to reach the shore; to be completed, finished, or accomplished; to go through and through, penetrate, permeate; to get well through, be successful, to succeed; to survive; to go away, be off, escape, attain refuge
and wār-pār, adv. On both sides (of a river, &c.); from end to end, through and through, quite through.
wety: Colloquial and regional variant of پر. Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀭𑀺 (pari), 𑀉𑀧𑁆𑀧𑀭𑀺 (uppari), from Sanskrit उपरि (upari, “above”).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. pā), Drinking; drinker (used as last member of comp., e.g. dwi-pa, s.m. lit. 'Twice-drinking'; the elephant).
more: s. m. 1. The wind. 2. Ӕolus the god of the wind. 3. In compos. (r. पा Preserve) A nourisher or protector. 4. (r. पा Drink) Drinking, drinker.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. pā), Protecting, guarding, cherishing, nourishing, ruling; — protector, &c. (used at the end of comp., e.g. bhū-pa, s.m. lit. 'Earth-protector'; sovereign, king).
adj. Five (used only in a few compounds, as pa-serī, s.f. A weight of five seers).
n. m. pa/?/khnī , n. f. ( Slang ). 1. Quarrelsome. 2. Foul-mouthed. Buṛī pakhnī hai, sāre din luṛtī rahtī hai .
n. m. A door rug.
more: (پا Foot, and انداز part. pass. of انداختن To throw) s. m. A carpet spread at the entrance of a room for cleaning the feet on.

def: n. m. A servant. Bandī ek kī pā-band hai, sāt ghar nahīṅ jhāṅktī . ( Demī-monde ). I am a servant of one master, I dont show my nose in seven different places. (The popular superstition is that a cat takes her kittens to seven different places before they open their eyes). pā-band , adj. Bound; restrained; fettered. Woh kisī mazhab kā pā-band nahīṅ hai. pā-band honā , v. n. 1. To be bound, fettered, etc. 2. To be bound or guided by; to adhere to; conform to; observe (a rule); follow; abide by.
more: 1. s. m. A rope with which the forefeet of a horse are tied. (Sans. पादबंध ). 2. adj. Clogged, fettered, bound. پابندهونا pā-band-honā, v. n. ( Met. ) To be married.

def: n. f. 1. The state of being bound. 2. Check; restraint; restriction; control; rule; observance; practice. Pā-bandī ek kī bhalī . Prov. One master is best.
v.t. = pabeṛnā, q.v. v.t. To throw, cast, throw about, to scatter (seed, &c.), to sow.
v.t. To throw, cast, throw about, to scatter (seed, &c.), to sow.
v. n. To sow broadcast.
s.m. = pavī, q.v. adj. & s.m. Purifying, purificatory; — who or what purifies, purifier.
s.f. Wick; pledget, &c. ( = battī, q.v. s.f. Wick, candle, light, lamp; match; slip of bamboo, &c.; bundle of bamboos; tent or bougie; stick (of sealing-wax); twisted folds of a turban
subs: (-meṅ), To put a candle in the candlestick; to raise the wick (of a lamp)
(-ko), To show a light; to apply a match (to); to set alight, to burn.

def: = H پبترا पबित्रा pabitrā, See pavitra and pavitrā.
more: adj. Pure, holy, undefiled, nice, clean.

def: = H پبتر पबित्र pabitr, See pavitra and pavitrā.
more: s. m. A brāhmanical thread, a string of silken beads, a knotted piece of kusha grass used in religious ceremonies of the Hindūs.

def: s. f. A ring of kusha grass (Poa cynosuroides), or of gold, silver, or copper, worn on the ring-finger and fore-finger by Hindūs during religious worship.
(پا Foot, and part. act. of بوسيدن To kiss) part. or s. m. Kissing the feet, i. e. Adoring, worshipping, revering. پابوس هونا pā-bos-honā, v. n. To worship, revere, adore.
s. f. Kissing the feet; worship, reverence, adoration. پابوسي کرنا pā-bosī-karnā, v. a. To worship, to reverence.
s. m. See پابوش pā-bos.
adj. = pāṅć, q.v. adj. Five; equal to, or as good as, five; sharp, astute
subs: s.m. Toll of five chaṭāṅk in a rupee's worth of rice or paddy (the toll is levied in large cities to defray the expenses of the inspectors of weighmen stationed in the bazars to prevent fraud in the weight and measure of commodities sold therein)
s.f. lit. 'Fives and sevens'; sixes and sevens, confusion, perplexity
(paṅća karma), s.m. The five actions of the body, viz. 1˚ Vomiting; 2˚ Evacuation by bleeding; 3˚ Evacuation by stool; 4˚ Diseased excretion by fæces or purging; 5˚ Blowing the nose
adj. The five, all five; — pāṅćoṅ bhāʼī, s.m. The five Pānḍav princes, viz. Yudhishṭhir, Bhīm-sen, Arjun, Nakul, and Sahdeo
(paṅća-bhūtātmā), s.m. lit. 'Consisting of the five primary elements'; a human being

s.m. The five articles of clothing, viz., 1˚ the pagṛī or turban; 2˚ the angarkhā or coat; 3˚ the pāʼe-jāma or trousers; 4˚ the du-paṭṭā or sheet or mantle; and 5˚ the rūmāl, or handkerchief; a complete suit of clothes
s.m. The five arms or weapons, viz. 1˚ the ḍhāl or shield; 2˚ the talwār or sword; 3˚ the barćhī or spear; 4˚ the tīr or arrow; and 5˚ the kamān or bow.

def: s.m. Emerald.
s.f. Support, countenance, protection, defence; partisanship ( = paćh; ḥimāyat); prejudice, bigotry; pertinacity, obstinate adherence (to, -kī).
more: n. f. S. पक्ष side. Prejudice; bigotry. See پاس (3). 1. A lock of hair. 2. Bandage. 3. A lesson. 4. A side of a bed. bāt kī pach , n. f. Obstinate adherence to one's opinion. Apnī bāt kī pach sab ko paṛ jātī hai. pach'chī; A. mutaٴassib , n. m. One who espouses the cause of another; a bigot.
adj. In comp. Cont. of پانچ five. pach-pakvānī , n. f. Five different sweetmeats served at a feast:— Khajlā, Khurmā, Imarti, Laḍḍū , and Kachaurī .

def: adj. Cooking, baking, roasting; digesting (used at the end of comp.).
adj. Five (used in comp. for pāṅć): pać-pakwānī, s.f. Five kinds of sweetmeats served at a feast (viz. khajtā; khurma; imratī; laḍḍū; kaćaurī)
subs: adj. & s.m. Five-sided, pentagonal; — a five-sided figure, a pentagon ( = muḵẖammas)
s.m. = pać-ras, s.m. Bell-metal, a kind of bronze
pać rangī, adj. Of five different colours
s.m. A horse whose four legs are half white and that has also white spots on the head
pać-khanā, adj. & s.m. Consisting of five parts or divisions (a house, &c.); — a five-storied house; five rooms, or spaces, in a row
s.m. A game played (by boys) with five pebbles
adj. & s.m. Consisting of five rows or strings (a necklace, &c.); — a necklace of five strings
s.m. A digestive composed of five kinds of salt
pać-maḥlā, adj. (f. -ī), & s.m. Consisting of five stories, rooms, or apartments (a house); — a five-storied house ( = pać-khanā)
adj. & s.m. Mixed, confused, unsorted, heterogeneous; — a mixture composed of five different ingredients; mixture, mess, hodge-podge
paćotrā (S. panća+uttara), s.m. Duty, or commission, of five per cent.
adj. Five hands high; very tall.

def: s.m. Sheep's foot; foot.
s.f. The act of cooking; cooking.

adj. & s.m. Cooking, concocting, aiding or effecting digestion, digestive, solvent, stomachic, tonic; causing to ripen; — the bile (which assists in digestion); a digestive, stomachic, solvent; — a cook.
more: s. m. 1. A digestive, a stomachick, a solvent. 2. A cook.
s. m. Cow-dung dried for fuel.

def: s.m. Cowdung dried for fuel (syn. uplā)
subs: s.m. Dry dung (of animals) found in fields and jungles (syn. kanḍe).

def: adj. & s.m. Cooking; — a cook.
adj. Aiding or promoting digestion.
s.m. A species of Curcuma, C. aromatica, or C. xanthorrhiza.

adj. & s.m. Ripening, maturing, suppurative; digestive, &c. ( = pāćak, q.v.); — a medicinal preparation, an infusion of various drugs (chiefly carminatives or gentle stimuli) given to bring the vitiated humours in fever, &c. to maturity; a decoction, sort of diet-drink; the bile, &c. ( = pāćak)
more: adj. S. पाचन from पच to cook. Digestive; solvent; stomachic; tonic. Soṅṭh, kachrī pāchan ,! Delhi street cry. pāchan , n. m. A medicinal preparation; a compound of various drugs, chiefly carminatives or gentle stimuli; the bile which assists digestion. Pāchak hai pachlonā, jis meṅ sabhī raṅg salonā, I to jhāre chāroṅ konā, jehu khāë to hokhe sonā . Street cry. Of five salts is this pāchak made, a tasty composition. Who takes it down is cleaned out quite, bright gold grows his complexion.
s. m. A digestive. See S. پاچک pāchak.

def: s.m. The act of cooking, cooking; becoming entirely cooked; ripening; putrefying; anything that cooks or matures; a frying-pan.
v.t. To digest; to assimilate; to cause to rot, to rot, to ferment; to spend, or to do away with the property of another dishonestly or wrongfully; to embezzle
more: v. a. S. पच् to cook. See پچنا v. n.
(trans. of پچنا) v. a. To digest, to cause to rot, to rot, to ferment.

def: s.m. A sort of diet-drink; borax.
adj. Ninety-five.
s.f. A species of myrobalan, Terminalia chebula.
part. adj. Fit to be cooked, &c. (see paćan).
s.m. Digestion, assimilation; &c. (see paćānā).
adj. Fifty.
more: adj. S. पञ्चाशत्, Pr. paṇṇāsā . Fifty.

def: s.m. A weight or scale of fifty rupees or tolās.
more: n. m. A weight or soale of 50 Rupees or tolās .

def: adj. Eighty-five.
adj. Fiftieth.
s.f. The stomach, the contents of the stomach, the entrails; the digestive organs; a digestive.
more: n. f. 1. The stomach; the digestive organs. 2. A digestive.

def: s.m. Digestion, assimilation; &c. (see paćānā).

ety: S. पक्ष+र; or from rt. पच् 'to spread'
def: s.m. A piece of wood driven between the handle and head of an axe or adze, &c., to make the handle fast; a wedge; a slip of wood used to fill up a crevice or opening
more: n. f. See اَرَ (6 to 9). A wedge; a slip of wood inserted to fill up a crack or opening. pachchar aṛānā yā lagānā , v. a. See aṛānā , 1—3. To insert a slip of wood, etc. in an opening. pachchar mārnā , v. a. See bhāṅjī mārṇā , v. a.
s. f. A wedge. پچرمارنا pachchar mārnā, To tease, to harass, to distress.
subs: paććar lagānā (-meṅ), To insert a slip of wood (into a crack or opening), to put in a wedge
v.t. To obstruct, thwart, frustrate, put a spoke in the wheel (of); to tease, harass, distress.

def: adj. & s.m. Western; — the West; western country, country to the west.
more: n. m. S. पश्चिम The West ( magrib ); the country to the west.
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi پچھم (pchm /⁠paccham⁠/) (c. 1736),, a semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit पश्चिम (paścima).

def: adj. & s.m. Western; — a native of the west.
s.m. Adherent, ally, assistant, favourer; advocate, patron; a bird (cf. paććī).
more: n. f. H. पुच्छ a tail. A tax on cattle. ( lit . on tails).
s. m. 1. An ally, an assistant, favourer, an advocate, a patron. 2. A bird.

def: adj. Adherent, joined, attached, sticking; fixed, firm, rooted, stable; (in Music) in unison
more: adj. H. pakkā firm. 1. Attached; joined; sticking; adherent. 2. Fixed; rooted; firm; stable. 3. ( Mus .) In unison. pachchī-kārī , n. f. Mosaic; a tesselated pavement. /?/hchī honā , v. n. 1. To be joined; dovetailed, glued, attached; to adhere; stick to. 2. To be strongly attached to one; to be hand and glove with. 3. ( Mus .) To be in unison.
adj. H. pisā ground. Mangled; bruised; crushed; squashed. Paīye tale ā-kar pāoṅ pichchī ho gayā .

adj. 1. Adherent, sticking, joined. 2. ( met. in musick) In unison. پچي کاري pachchī-kārī, s. f. 1. Mosaick. 2. Patching or darning of damaged cloth. پچي هونا pachchī honā, 1. To be stuck together as with glue. 2. To be in unison. 3. To be strongly attached by love.
subs: s.f. Mosaic; a tesselated pavement; patching, darning

v.n. To be joined; to be stuck together; to adhere, stick (to); to be stuck, jammed or squeezed (between), to be wedged; to be strongly attached (to), to be thick (with), be hand and glove (with); (in Music) to be in unison or harmony.

def: s.m. Kicking out with the hind legs (a horse).
s.m. Scarifying; incision; inoculation; incision made in the poppy head for the opium to ooze out.
s.m. Wing; feather; side, part, assistance, adherence, countenance, support, protection, defence; partiality (see pać); the half of a lunar month, a fortnight comprising fifteen days.
(see پچنا) v. a. To digest, to rot.
s.m. The West, &c. = paććham or paććhim, q.v. adj. & s.m. Western; — the West; western country, country to the west.

s.f. (prov. or local) = pahćān, q.v. s.f. Knowledge, acquaintance, ascertainment, recognition, experience, discrimination, discernment; distinguishing mark, characteristic; indication, token, sign (also written paićhān); — an acquaintance ( = jān-pahćān).

s.f. (prov. or local) = pahćān, q.v. s.f. Knowledge, acquaintance, ascertainment, recognition, experience, discrimination, discernment; distinguishing mark, characteristic; indication, token, sign (also written paićhān); — an acquaintance ( = jān-pahćān).
more: s. f. (dakh. from پچهاننا) Knowledge, acquaintance.

def: s.m. = paćhāṅ; paććham, q.v. s.m. The West, &c. = paććham or paććhim, q.v. adj. & s.m. Western; — the West; western country, country to the west.
v. a. (dakh.) See پچهاننا pachhānnā.
v.t. corr. of pahćannā, q.v. v.t. To perceive, know, comprehend, understand; to recognize, identify; to distinguish, discern, discriminate
more: (see پـﮩچاننا) v. a. To know, to be acquainted with, to recognise.

def: s.f. The act of winnowing ( = paćhoṛ, q.v.). s.f. The act of winnowing.
more: (from پچهاڙنا) s. f. 1. A fall or throwing down. 2. The act of winnowing. پچهاڙکهانا pachhāṛ khānā, To fall down, to suffer pain.

ety: S. पश्चात्+कारी; or v.n. of paćhāṛnā
def: s.f. Falling on the back; a fall on the back, a throw; falling back in a swoon
subs: or paćhāṛeṅ, khānā, v.n. To fall backwards; to be thrown on the back; to fall back in a swoon; to throw oneself down repeatedly in grief or pain, to writhe in agony; to drop into the grave.

def: v.t. To give (one) a fall, to throw down on the back, to dash down, to lay prostrate; to overpower, overcome, subdue, abase.
more: (trans. of پچهڙنا) v. a. To throw down, to abase, to conquer.

def: v.n. To fall back, to fall, tumble down; to be backward; to remain or lag behind; to fail, fall short (of)
more: v. n. To fall, to tumble down. پچهڙجانا pachhaṛ jānā, v. n. To be overcome.
subs: v.n. To be thrown; to be overcome.

def: adj. Fiftieth.
adj. Seventy-five.
adj. S. पश्चिम, Pr. pachchhimam . Western. pachhmī , n. m. A native of the west.
adj. Twenty-fifth.

adv. = pīćhe, q.v. s.f. The scum of boiled rice and of other grain; the gum of the silk-cotton tree; the venomous saliva of a snake; a multitude, heap; the calf of the leg; a sheath, coat or cover; the areca nut, betel nut; a line, row, range; a diseased affection of a horse's feet; a plantain, Musa sapientum; the sisu tree, Dalbergia sisu; armour, a sort of cuirass or jacket.
s.f. = H پچهيلوا पछेलवा paćhelwā, s.m. A kind of bracelet (worn with ćūrīs; the ćūrīs are worn nearest to the hand).
s.m. = H پچهيلی पछेली paćhelī, s.f. A kind of bracelet (worn with ćūrīs; the ćūrīs are worn nearest to the hand).

n. m. 1. Inoculation. 2. The incision made in the poppy head for the opium to ooze out. 3. Kicking out with the hind legs (a horse).
more: (from پاچهنا) s. m. The act of inoculating, inoculation.

def: s. m. 1. A wing, feather. 2. The half of a lunar month, or a fortnight comprising fifteen days. 3. Side, party, assistance, adherence. 4. Partiality.

v. a. 1. To throw on one's back; dash down. 2. To overpower; overcome; conquer. Mathurā meṅ jā Kans pachhāṛe, Māre asur, miṭe dukh bhāre . 3. To prostrate; throw on one's back; lay up. Bukhār ne pachhāṛ dīyā .

v. n. See پچهارَنا v. a. Nahīṅ hai zor jinhoṅ meṅ woh kushtī laṛte haiṅ, Jo zor-wāle haiṅ woh āp se pachhaṛte haiṅ . The world. Nazīr. To remain or lag behind; to be backward; to fail. Kahāṅ the jo kām se pachhaṛ gaë. pachhaṛ jānā , v. n. To be overcome.
adv. Behind, after, afterwards, in the absence. See پيچهي.

def: n. m. pach-laṛī , n. f. A necklace consisting of five rows or strings. Lagā dhugdhugī, pach-laṛā, sat-laṛā, Sarāsar gale husn uske paṛā . Mīr Hasan.
s. f. A necklace consisting of five rows or strings.
more: s. f. An ornament for the neck, five rows of small round gold beads threaded on silk ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

def: n. m. See پچکهنا (1.)
more: ( पंच Five, and محل Mansion) adj. Of five stories (a house).

ety: fr. S. प्रच्छो; rt. छो with प्र
def: v.t. To scarify, cup, lance, puncture, to inoculate, to make an incision (in).
more: v. a. 1. To puncture; prick; inoculate. 2. To make a notch with a sharp instrument on a wall, as the dāiṅ (witch) is supposed to do, so that the same cuts may break out on the body of the person whose injury is contemplated.

ety: fr. S. प्रच्छी, rt. छी, with प्र
def: s.m. The act of scarifying, tattooing, cupping, or lancing; inoculation, vaccination; — paćhnā, s.m. A scarificator; lancet
more: n. m. 1. The act of scarifying; tattooing; inoculation; vaccination; cupping. 2. A scarificator. 3. The scarifying of the poppy head to procure opium.
s. m. 1. The act of scarifying (tattooing). 2. Inoculation. 3. A scarificator (name of an instrument).
subs: (-ko), To scarify, cup, lance; to make incisions in a poppy-head (to procure opium).

ety: fr. S. प्रच्छी, rt. छी, with प्र

def: s.f. The act of winnowing.
s.f. The chaff which flies off in winnowing.
v.t. To winnow (with a basket used for the purpose; syn. phaṭaknā).
more: v. a. To winnow with a basket used for this purpose. See پهٿکنا phaṭaknā.

def: n. m. Five per cent commission. pach-hatta , adj. Five hands high. pach-hatta jvān , n. m. A tall man.
more: s. m. A duty of five per cent. on merchandise.

def: adj. Of five different colors. Lāl palaṅg pe pachraṅg takye . Song.
v.n. To regret, repent, rue, grieve.
more: v. n. S. पश्चात्तापन from पश्चात् after, तापन burning. To repent; rue; grieve; repine; deplore. 1. Ujjal phūl dekh seṅbhal kā bhiram gayā sūhā, Phari toṛ-ke choṅch jo mārī pachhtāyā sūhā . The red flowers of the cotton tree the parrot did deceive, He drove his beak into the pod which made him sorely grieve. 2. Jis ne jaiso dīyo hai, tis ne taiso pāyo, Tū kan ke māre phirat kyoṅ man meṅ pachhtāyo? Dohā. As in his former birth each one did give he hath received, Your wanderings for bread are vain, in vain your spirit's grieved. 3. Binā bichāre jo kare, so pāchhe pachhtāë, Kām bigāṛe āpno, jag meṅ hot haṅsāë . Girdhar Kuṅdalyā. Who acts before he thinks will surely afterwards repent, Who mars his own work is but food for general laughter meant.
v. n. To regret, repent, rue, grieve.

def: s. m. Repentance: see پچهتاوا pachhtāwā.
s.m. Regret, remorse, repentance, penitence, compunction, contrition, concern, grief, sorrow
subs: (-par), To regret, to suffer remorse (on account of)

def: v.n. = paćhtānā, q.v. v.n. To regret, repent, rue, grieve.

s.m. Regret, remorse, repentance, penitence, compunction, contrition, concern, grief, sorrow
more: n. m. S. पश्चात्ताप Pr. pachchhattāvo repentance. Repentance; penitence; contrition; compunction; remorse; self-reproach. Rakh pachhtāvā kuchh nahiṅ, bech pachhtāvā achchhā . Prov. Better repent you sold too soon than repent you did not sell in time.
s. m. Regret, penitence, contrition, concern, compunction, grief, sorrow, remorse.
subs: (-par), To regret, to suffer remorse (on account of)

def: s. m. A kind of cloth.
s.f. The west wind.
more: n. f. The west wind. Pachhvā chale, khetī phale . Prov. When the wind's from the west, the harvest is best. ('When the wind's in the west, the weather's at the best').

def: adj. Twenty-five.
s.f. A game played (on a kind of chess-board or cloth) with cowries for dice (and so named from the highest throw, which is twenty-five).
more: n. f. A game played on a kind of cloth chess board with kauris for dice, and so named from the highest throw, which is twenty-five.
s. f. A game played with kauṛīs instead of dice, and so named from the highest throw, which is twenty five.

def: adj. Twenty-fifth.
s.f. A conjunction of five unlucky stars; six days from Śrāvaṇ to Revatī, on which days several kinds of work are unlawful.
more: n. f. S. पञ्चषा A conjunction of five unlucky stars; five days, from Shravan to Revatī, on which days certain kinds of work are unlawful ( panchak ).
s. f. Six days, from श्रवण to रेवती , on which days several kinds of work are unlawful.

def: = H پچلڙا पचलड़ा pać-laṛā, = H پچلونا पचलोना pać-lonā, See s.v. pać, 'five.'
more: n. m. 1. A fivestoried house. 2. Five rooms in a row. pach-khūṅṭ , P. panj-goshah , A. mukhhammas , (Math) . A pentagon.
adj. Consisting of five parts or divisions (a house, &c.).

def: = H پچکهنا पचखना pać-khanā, = H پچلونا पचलोना pać-lonā, See s.v. pać, 'five.'
= H پچکهنا पचखना pać-khanā, = H پچلڙا पचलड़ा pać-laṛā, See s.v. pać, 'five.'
more: n. m. A digestive composed of five kinds of salt, har, pīpal , etc.
s. m. A medicine composed of five kinds of salt.

def: adj. & s.m. See s.v. pać, 'five.' adj. = pāṅć, q.v. adj. Five; equal to, or as good as, five; sharp, astute
more: n. m. A mixture composed of five different ingredients; hence, a mixture, mess, hodge podge, omnium gatherum. pach-mel , adj. Mixed; heterogenous; promiscuous; unsorted; confused.
subs: s.m. Toll of five chaṭāṅk in a rupee's worth of rice or paddy (the toll is levied in large cities to defray the expenses of the inspectors of weighmen stationed in the bazars to prevent fraud in the weight and measure of commodities sold therein)
s.f. lit. 'Fives and sevens'; sixes and sevens, confusion, perplexity
(paṅća karma), s.m. The five actions of the body, viz. 1˚ Vomiting; 2˚ Evacuation by bleeding; 3˚ Evacuation by stool; 4˚ Diseased excretion by fæces or purging; 5˚ Blowing the nose
adj. The five, all five; — pāṅćoṅ bhāʼī, s.m. The five Pānḍav princes, viz. Yudhishṭhir, Bhīm-sen, Arjun, Nakul, and Sahdeo
(paṅća-bhūtātmā), s.m. lit. 'Consisting of the five primary elements'; a human being

s.m. The five articles of clothing, viz., 1˚ the pagṛī or turban; 2˚ the angarkhā or coat; 3˚ the pāʼe-jāma or trousers; 4˚ the du-paṭṭā or sheet or mantle; and 5˚ the rūmāl, or handkerchief; a complete suit of clothes
s.m. The five arms or weapons, viz. 1˚ the ḍhāl or shield; 2˚ the talwār or sword; 3˚ the barćhī or spear; 4˚ the tīr or arrow; and 5˚ the kamān or bow.

def: s.m. = pāćan; — v.n. = paćnā; — and v.t. = pāćhnā; qq.v. adj. & s.m. Ripening, maturing, suppurative; digestive, &c. ( = pāćak, q.v.); — a medicinal preparation, an infusion of various drugs (chiefly carminatives or gentle stimuli) given to bring the vitiated humours in fever, &c. to maturity; a decoction, sort of diet-drink; the bile, &c. ( = pāćak)
